title: AiEvidenceEnvelopeV1
description: Tamper-evident evidence envelope for an AI generation call. Designed to be canonicalized (RFC 8785 / JCS), hashed, and timestamped (RFC 3161). Sensitive payloads are referenced by hash so the envelope itself can be retained without retaining PII.

schemaName.description: Stable schema identifier. Always 'AiEvidenceEnvelope' for this family.

schemaVersion.description: Major version of the envelope schema. Breaking changes increment this.

evidenceId.description: Stable identifier for this evidence record. Generated by the producer; never reused.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp the envelope was assembled. Distinct from the RFC 3161 genTime in proofs.

category.description: Free-form classifier for why the AI call was made (e.g. 'classification', 'summarization', 'advisory').

type.description: Whether the call was driven by a human user, a backend service, or autonomous system.

id.description: Stable identifier for the actor (e.g. user UUID, service principal). NOT the actor's name or PII.

tenantId.description: Optional tenant the actor belongs to.

displayHint.description: Optional non-PII display hint (e.g. role, department).

correlationId.description: Tracing/correlation id linking this evidence to the parent business transaction.

parentEvidenceId.description: Optional link to a prior envelope this one continues from.

name.description: Model identifier as reported by the provider (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7').

version.description: Provider-reported model version or build, if any.

deploymentId.description: Optional deployment-specific identifier (e.g. Azure deployment name).

parameters.description: Sampling parameters used (temperature, top_p, max_tokens, ...). Recorded verbatim.

prompt.description: The prompt as a payload reference (hash + ref) or inline content. Use a reference for PII-bearing prompts.

inputs.description: Additional structured inputs (tools, function arguments, attachments) used by the call.

retrievedContext.description: RAG / retrieval-augmented context chunks fed into the model. Each entry SHOULD be a hash reference rather than inline content.

sourceTrace.description: Provenance trail: where did the retrieved context originate? Useful for downstream auditability.

output.description: The model's response. Use a reference for PII-bearing outputs.

outputArtifacts.description: Generated artifacts beyond the primary output (e.g. structured JSON, generated files).

policyMetadata.description: Applied policies, redactions, and content filters at the time of the call.

redactionPolicy.description: Identifier of the redaction policy applied (e.g. 'pii-redaction-v3').

canonicalization.description: Canonicalization scheme used. v1 supports only RFC 8785 (JCS).

envelopeHash.description: Hash of the canonical envelope with the integrity.envelopeHash and proofs fields *removed* before canonicalization. See spec/README.md.

proofs.description: Cryptographic proofs over the envelopeHash. v1 supports RFC 3161 timestamp tokens. Multiple proofs MAY be present (e.g. timestamp chain, future signatures).

alg.description: Hash algorithm. SHA-256 is the default; SHA-512 is supported for parity with Sigill auto-TSA.

sizeBytes.description: Optional: size of the input bytes that were hashed.

payloadRef.description: A payload represented either inline (small, non-sensitive) or by hash reference (sensitive or large). Exactly one of 'inline' or 'hash' MUST be present.

ref.description: Stable identifier the verifier uses to look up the external payload bytes. REQUIRED when 'hash' is set.

contentType.description: MIME type of the underlying payload (e.g. 'text/plain', 'application/json').

encoding.description: How the payload was encoded prior to hashing. 'utf-8' for text, 'base64' if you want to record it verbatim inline, 'binary' for files.

inline.description: The payload itself, included verbatim. ONLY use for non-PII content. Mutually exclusive with 'hash'.

hash.description: Hash of the external payload. Mutually exclusive with 'inline'.

metadata.description: Optional structured metadata about the payload (length, language, role, ...).

tsaName.description: Human-readable name of the TSA that produced this proof, as reported by Sigill.

genTime.description: TSA-asserted time, copied from the TSR for convenience. Authoritative value lives inside tsrBase64.

serial.description: TSR serial number, copied for convenience.

qualified.description: Whether this is an eIDAS Qualified timestamp.

policyOid.description: Policy OID asserted by the TSA, present for qualified timestamps.
